display_name: Benedetto 17" Archtop â€” Spanish Rope Binding
source: Custom design spec. Body/neck geometry from models/benedetto_17.json (AI-extracted Benedetto blueprints). Spanish rope binding added as custom trim package. Binding runs body perimeter, up the neck, and around the headstock.

venetian_cutaway.description: Benedetto 17" with Venetian cutaway and full-wrap Spanish rope binding

binding.note: Rope binding runs both edges of the fretboard from nut to body joint and continues along the neck heel
Rope binding wraps the full headstock perimeter, continuous with neck binding through the nut transition

non_cutaway.description: Benedetto 17" symmetrical (no cutaway) with full-wrap Spanish rope binding

binding.note: Spanish rope binding is continuous: body perimeter -> up the neck edges -> around the headstock. Traditional rope pattern uses alternating ebony/maple veneers cut at angle to create twisted rope illusion.

side_binding.note: Plain maple side binding connecting top and back rope purfling

body_to_neck.note: Rope binding miters at the heel joint where body binding meets neck binding. Requires precise 45-degree miter at the heel cap.

neck_to_headstock.note: Rope binding transitions at the nut from fretboard edge to headstock perimeter. Miter joint masked by nut overhang.

rosette_note: Archtop guitars do not have rosettes â€” soundholes are F-holes, not round soundholes

pickups.note: Classic Benedetto configuration â€” single floating humbucker on pickguard bracket, no body routing required

dxf_assets.note: DXF assets from Benedetto blueprint vectorizer extraction (AI vision, 2026-01-30)
dxf_assets._note: DXF assets pending creation from Benedetto blueprints in Guitar Plans/Benedetto/

spanish_rope_binding_fabrication.overview: Spanish rope binding is fabricated using the traditional matrix method. Alternating strips of ebony and maple veneer are glued in a stack, cross-cut into chips, then reassembled with column offsets to create the twisted rope illusion. The binding strip is then bent to follow the body contour, neck edges, and headstock perimeter.
steps: 1. Prepare veneer strips: 5 rows per classic_rope_5x9 matrix â€” ebony and maple, 1.0mm wide, 0.6mm thick
2. Glue strip stack in order defined by matrix rows
3. Cross-cut cured stack into chips at 2.0mm intervals (chip_length_mm)
4. Reassemble chips following column_sequence [1,2,2,1,3,4,5,4,3] to produce rope twist
5. Glue reassembled strip onto backing tape for handling
6. Heat-bend the rope strip to conform to body perimeter curve (soak + bending iron)
7. Rout purfling channel: 6.0mm wide Ã— 2.0mm deep along body top/back perimeter
8. Glue rope strip + outer maple + inner ebony into channel
9. Repeat for neck edges: narrower channel (2.5mm wide Ã— 1.5mm deep), miter at heel joint
10. Continue around headstock perimeter: miter at nut transition, follow headstock outline
11. Final: scrape and level binding flush with body/neck/headstock surfaces

critical_joints.note: Where body binding meets neck binding at the heel
Where neck binding transitions to headstock binding
Venetian cutaway requires bending the rope strip to ~15mm radius
Body waist has tightest standard curve, ~50mm radius

tools_required: Purfling channel router with 6.0mm and 2.5mm straight bits
CNC router for precise channel following body DXF outline
Cross-cut sled for chip cutting at 2.0mm intervals

notes: Scale length: 647.7mm (25.5 inches) â€” Benedetto standard
Body dimensions from AI-extracted Benedetto blueprints (models/benedetto_17.json)
DXF body outline and F-holes extracted from vectorizer scan (COMPLETE status)
Spanish rope binding is continuous from body to neck to headstock â€” 3 miter joints total
Rope pattern uses classic_rope_5x9 preset from traditional pattern library
Archtop has no rosette â€” uses F-holes instead of round soundhole
Floating bridge and tailpiece â€” no bridge pin holes or saddle slot routing needed
Floating pickup on pickguard bracket â€” no pickup routing in body
Top and back are hand-graduated carved arches â€” CNC rough carving possible, final graduation by hand
European spruce top / European maple back and sides â€” traditional Benedetto wood selection
